etymology.description: semantic 亻(亻) (rén, "person") + phonetic 弗(弗) (fú) -> Buddha
etymology.interpretation: The character 佛 (fó, "Buddha") combines 亻(亻) (rén, "person") as the semantic part indicating a person, and 弗(弗) (fú) providing the phonetic element. The combination suggests the concept of a revered human figure, specifically referring to Buddha.

examples.chinese: 寺庙里供奉了一尊佛像(寺廟裏供奉了一尊佛像)。
examples.pinyin: Sìmiào lǐ gōngfèng le yī zūn fóxiàng.
examples.english: A statue of Buddha is enshrined in the temple.

examples.chinese: 他每天早上打坐冥想关于佛的智慧(他每天早上打坐冥想關於佛的智慧)。
examples.pinyin: Tā měitiān zǎoshang dǎzuò míngxiǎng guānyú Fó de zhìhuì.
examples.english: He meditates every morning on the wisdom of Buddha.

examples.chinese: 佛教在亚洲许多国家中非常流行(佛教在亞洲許多國家中非常流行)。
examples.pinyin: Fójiào zài Yàzhōu xǔduō guójiā zhōng fēicháng liúxíng.
examples.english: Buddhism is very popular in many Asian countries.
